lines.text: Can we just be honest?
These are the requirements
If you think you can be my one and only true love
You must promise to love me
And damn it, if you fuck me over
I will rip your fuckin' face apart
Step one
You must accept that I'm a little out my mind
Step two
This is a waste if you can't walk me down the finish line
Step three
Give me passion, don't make fun of my fashion
Step four
Give me more, give me more, more
If you can't handle a heart like mine
Don't waste your time with me
If you're not down to bleed, no, oh
If you can't handle the chokin', the bitin'
The lovin', the smotherin'
'Til you can't handle it no more, no more
Go home

children: She wants her sweetheart to actually put effort into keeping a good relationship.

39669118.fragment:
Step six
 If you can’t put in work, I don’t know what you think this fuckin’ is

children: She wants someone straightfoward and proud of the woman they have as their sweetheart, showing her off and their relationship. She doesn’t wants to be a secret.

32643517.fragment:
Step five
 You can’t be scared to show me off and hold my hand

children: Melanie is telling people: Line up or shut up. If you’re not her type, don’t waste your time with her.
Or: If you can’t handle a heart like mine/don’t waste your time with me, another lyric in the song.

32589973.fragment:
High school sweethearts, line up
 Not tryna waste my time
 High school sweethearts, shut up
 If you’re not my type
 High school sweethearts, line up
 Not tryna waste my time
 High school sweethearts, shut up
 If you’re not my type

children: Here, Melanie uses conflicting metaphors of up and down. “Get me up, yeah, get me high” refers to her partner making her feel happy and elated to be with them. “Get me high” could also imply using drugs, as she references weed especially a few times throughout K-12. In Lunchbox Friends, she addresses how she is judged for smoking weed, and wants a friend who will accept her for that. It seems reasonable to assume she would want the same in a romantic partner.
To “tie [someone] down” means to get married or settle with them in a committed relationship long-term. Melanie doesn’t want a fling, she wants a deep, committed, emotional relationship.

32248219.fragment:
Get me up, yeah, get me high
 Tie me down, don’t leave my side

children: Here Crybaby, (Melanie’s alter ego) is telling the listener, or whatever you like to call it, they should comfort her into having less suicidal thoughts. “Make me not wanna die” should give you a hint that she doesnt want you to make her feel like a piece of crap, but treat her with heart.
“Love me rough and let me fly” so basically, if you choose to be with her, make sure you love her with all you can so she’ll be able to have the feeling of a healthy relationship.

30261231.fragment:
Make me not wanna die
 Love me rough and let me fly

children: Melanie basically implies that love is a cruel game and there’s times where she and her lover might fight, furthermore implying that the moments where there’s a heated arguments are some of the best in a relationship – and if her lover can’t handle it then he shouldn’t even bother being with her. Calling back to her 2015 song Gingerbread Man “One who calls me crazy, never calls me baby, that’s the one I want” and her unreleased song Arts & Crafts “I’m not looking for perfection, I want a challenge”

29039697.fragment:
If you’re not down to bleed, no, oh
 If you can’t handle the chokin’, the bitin’
 The lovin’, the smotherin’
 ’Til you can’t handle it no more, no more
 Go home

children: Melanie – and by extension her character, Crybaby – says she wants more of the passion and love, and especially more respect for who she is.

29039690.fragment:
Step four
 Give me more, give me more, more

children: In this line Crybaby tells to her potential lover how there are rules to love that must be obeyed – calling back to her 2015 song “Gingerbread Man”

 These are the requirements
 If you think you can be my one and only true love
 You must promise to love me
